what_it_does: This file is a first-hand structural discovery report that catalogs and prioritizes Tim Geldard's personal theorem-bearing vaults, flags which have been mined versus remain unmined, and highlights a specific operational engine document that appears to instantiate the theorem as a company architecture.
observations: Five theorem-bearing vaults are listed with file/chunk counts, paths, and priority order; only relative-difference has been mined, while visual-dna, unification-vault, ulm-inventory, and vi-context-design are unmined.
Semantic search is down, so the survey is structural-only and read-only; the company session index is also corrupted.
relative-difference contains relation-kinds such as tims_verbatim and tims_direction, plus tag-axes including axis, face, body, layer, articulation, source, and round.

A list of deep formalizations is cataloged as articulation-types, including monodromy-structure, coupled-coalgebra-runtime, eigenpoint-lattice, universal-evaluator, and parametric-substrate-operation.
Tim's tagged verbatim articulations cover sequence, incompletion, self-application, anti-summary, and confidence-is-not-processing-authority, framed as the theorem applied to its own working rules.
The crown find, The Engine - Working Notes (Integrated), enumerates six axes and states that the structurally-required axis set is a property of the rule being run.
The file notes a seam among three axis counts: six axes from The Engine, four prime axes from a 2026-06-19 brain-wave, and a per-operation required set.
standouts: The crown find document is presented as the theorem already operationalized as the company architecture before/as it was built.
The decisive line that the rule declares its required axis positions collapses fixed axis lists into a per-operation coordinate.
Tim's own working rules (orphan relation, addressable incompletion, self-application, anti-summary) are read as the theorem applied to itself.
Four high-value vaults remain unmined, creating a recollection gap between personal vault work and the company corpus.
concerns: Findings are structural-only because semantic search is unavailable, so content claims about unmined vaults are inferred from folder names and metadata.
The report relies heavily on personal vaults outside the company corpus without a stated ingestion or validation path.
Axis counts are presented as unresolved and awaiting Tim adjudication, which leaves a core coordinate model unsettled.
Terms like 39 open seams and 30 open seams are used without local definition.
The boundary between theorem, engine, resolver, and company architecture is asserted rather than demonstrated within this file.
notes: A first-hand structural inventory of Tim's personal theorem-bearing vaults, flagging an unmined operational engine document as the likely base for the company universal-projection build.
questions: What is the exact content of the unmined vaults beyond folder-level metadata?
How will material from personal vaults be fed into and validated within the company recall corpus?
What formally distinguishes the six axes, the four-root axes, and the per-operation required axis set?
Is The Engine document current, superseded, or a candidate among several operationalization drafts?
What do the open seam counts measure and where are they recorded?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to map where Tim's theorem lives; the file also interprets priority and operational meaning, which is consistent with its build-prep context.
apparent_themes: Everything is a variable, including the axis set itself
Rule-declared required axes as the coordinate model
AI as units rather than agents
Render-for-cognition and visible incoherence as signal
Recollection gap between personal vaults and company corpus
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish that a pre-existing, formalized personal theorem and its operational engine document can serve as the foundation for the company's universal-projection system, and to direct mining work toward the unindexed vaults that hold it.
novelty: It treats an individual's personal note vaults as a formal theorem-bearing substrate and identifies a pre-build engine document as the corporate architecture's conceptual ancestor.
contribution: A prioritized map of candidate theorem sources, structural metadata for five vaults, a pointer to a detailed operational engine document, and a set of verbatim axes and working rules that any fused system would need to reconcile when building a universal-projection substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a 2026-06-20 structural discovery report listing Tim Geldard's personal theorem-bearing vaults, their metadata, unmined high-value folders, and a crown-find engine document that operationalizes the theorem as a company architecture.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to locate and prioritize the theorem sources that should form the base of a company universal-projection build.
